title: Rich Decadent Canadian Pepper Poutine
description: A rich, velvety Canadian dish featuring green peppers and a high-protein mother sauce base.
instructions: Combine 2 cups of all-purpose flour, 2 tablespoons of salt, and 1/4 cup of ice-cold water to create a thickening paste for the gravy.
In a large saucepan, combine 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil over medium heat.
Add 1 medium onion, diced, and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add 2 large cloves of garlic, minced, and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add 1 cup of all-purpose flour and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Gradually add 2 cups of beef broth, whisking continuously to avoid lumps.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to a simmer and cook until thickened, about 10 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried thyme and 1/2 teaspoon of paprika to the gravy and stir to combine.
In a separate pan, combine 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil over medium heat.
Add 1 large green pepper, sliced, and cook until tender, about 5 minutes.
Add 1 pound of high-protein cheese curds to the pan and cook until melted and golden brown, about 2 minutes.
To assemble the poutine, place 2 cups of French fries in the bottom of a serving dish, followed by the green pepper and cheese curd mixture, and finally the rich gravy.
